methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Inversion Space is a Series A-stage housing and construction company founded by Justin Fiaschetti and Austin Briggs. The company has raised $44M to date. The comparable universe for valuation consists of two private peers in the same sector—Cobod and Material Evolution—as no direct public comparables exist in this frontier technology domain.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable to this investment. Using total capital raised ($44M) as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A-to-subsequent-stage step-ups yields an implied valuation of $88M. This method reflects the expected value accretion from the completion of Series A milestones through the next financing round.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by availability of a single applicable methodology, supports a valuation range of $75M (low) to $101M (high), with a median of $88M. The narrow methodological foundation—relying exclusively on stage-progression rather than comparable multiples or DCF—represents the primary uncertainty. Validation would benefit from multiples-based analysis on the private peer set (Cobod and Material Evolution) if sufficient financial data becomes available. The range implies modest step-up from total capital deployed, consistent with typical market conditions for housing-tech infrastructure plays at this stage.
